process_name: Flavor-changing neutral-current top decay t -> u Z
implementation_difficulty_reason: MEDIUM to store the external upper limit, but HIGH for production-grade RS integration because the repo lacks electroweak top-FCNC matching, a tuZ decay-width/production interpretation, and top-sector flavor-rotation plumbing.

evidence: Required plan greps rerun on 2026-05-16; no source-code implementation of t -> u Z or tZu found.

Targeted grep across quarkConstraints, qcd, flavorConstraints, neutrinos, yukawa, warpConfig, solvers, scanParams, tests found only generic FCNC prose/comments in quarkConstraints/PAPER_0710_1869.md:35 and tests/test_paper_couplings.py:258, not an observable implementation.
grep_commands: rg -n "epsilon_K|epsilon_k|Delta m_K|DELTA_M_K|evaluate_delta_mk|B_d|b_d|B_s|b_s|D0|d_mix|mu.?e.?gamma|muToEGamma|BR_LIMIT|MEG" quarkConstraints flavorConstraints scanParams neutrinos yukawa qcd warpConfig solvers tests docs -g "!*.ipynb"

rg -n "b.?->.?s|X_s|K\\*|R_K|R_D|B_s.*mu|pi.*nu|nu.*nubar|epsilon.?prime|eps.?prime|t.?->|Z.?->|h.?->|EDM|dipole|conversion|tau.?->|mu.?->.?3e" quarkConstraints flavorConstraints scanParams neutrinos yukawa qcd warpConfig solvers tests docs -g "!*.ipynb"

rg -n "MODERN_PHENOMENOLOGY_SYSTEM_IDS|DEFAULT_DELTA_F2_INPUTS|evaluate_bd_mixing|evaluate_bs_mixing|evaluate_d0_mixing|check_mu_to_e_gamma" quarkConstraints flavorConstraints scanParams tests docs -g "!*.ipynb"
rg -n "t.?->.?u.?Z|t.?->.?Z.?u|tuZ|tZu|tZq|FCNC|flavou?r-changing|flavor changing|Z.?u" quarkConstraints qcd flavorConstraints neutrinos yukawa warpConfig solvers scanParams tests -g "!*.ipynb"

sections.process:
\textbf{Standard notation:} \(\mathcal{B}(t\to uZ)\).
This entry catalogs the flavor-changing neutral-current top decay \(t\to uZ\)
in the top/Higgs/electroweak family.  It covers direct LHC searches for a top
quark coupled to an up quark and a \(Z\) boson, including interpretations that
combine \(t\bar t\) events with one FCNC top decay and single-top production in
association with a \(Z\) boson.
sections.pdg_prose:
The source-of-truth numerical entries are the
\texttt{pdg\_or\_equivalent} block in \texttt{T002.yaml}.  The current PDG
2025 top-quark listing quotes the ATLAS AAD 23AS limits in units of
\(10^{-3}\) (\texttt{PDG2025TopFCNCTZQ}):
\[
  \mathcal{B}(t\to Zu) < 6.2\times 10^{-5}
\]
for the left-handed \(tuZ\) benchmark
(\texttt{PDG2025:T002:tZu\_left}) and
\[
  \mathcal{B}(t\to Zu) < 6.6\times 10^{-5}
\]
for the right-handed benchmark (\texttt{PDG2025:T002:tZu\_right}), both at
95\% CL.  The same PDG table records the CMS SIRUNYAN 17E comparison limit
\(\mathcal{B}(t\to Zu)<0.022\%\), also at 95\% CL
(\texttt{CMS2017:T002:tZu}).  The local PDG snapshot is
\texttt{flavor\_catalog/references/T002/pdg\_2025\_top\_fcnc\_tzq.txt}.
sections.relevance:
In warped models with bulk fermions, non-universal zero-mode profiles and
mass-basis rotations can induce flavor off-diagonal neutral-current \(Z\)
couplings.  The \(t\to uZ\) channel probes the first--third generation
up-sector rotation and is therefore complementary to charm and neutral-meson
constraints.  In anarchic RS language it is sensitive to the same profile and
Yukawa structures that control \(u\)-\(c\)-\(t\) mixing, while remaining a
direct top observable rather than a low-energy hadronic matrix-element
constraint.
sections.post_2008:
The CFW reference point is arXiv:0804.1954, which framed generic
warped-flavor bounds before the full LHC top-FCNC program
(\texttt{CsakiFalkowskiWeiler2008WarpedFlavor}).  Shortly after it, the
RS-specific Casagrande--Goertz--Haisch--Neubert--Pfoh analysis included
\(t\to c(u)Z\), noting that \(t\to uZ\) is typically about two orders of
magnitude below \(t\to cZ\) in the minimal RS model
(\texttt{Casagrande2008:T002:RS\_suppression}).  Since then the key
experimental development is flavor-separated LHC sensitivity.  CMS used
\(19.7~\mathrm{fb}^{-1}\) at \(\sqrt{s}=8~\mathrm{TeV}\)
(\texttt{CMS2017:T002:dataset}) and found
\(\mathcal{B}(t\to Zu)<0.022\%\) (\texttt{CMS2017:T002:tZu}).  ATLAS later
used \(139~\mathrm{fb}^{-1}\) at \(\sqrt{s}=13~\mathrm{TeV}\)
(\texttt{ATLAS2023:T002:dataset}), combining single-top \(tZ\) production and
\(t\bar t\) FCNC-decay topologies, and improved the \(t\to Zu\) reach to the
\(6\times10^{-5}\) level (\texttt{ATLAS2023:T002:tZu\_left},
\texttt{ATLAS2023:T002:tZu\_right}).
sections.validity:
Experimentally this is a clean 95\% CL upper limit; there is no Standard Model
signal at the observable level.  Aguilar-Saavedra's benchmark SM estimate is
\(\mathcal{B}(t\to uZ)\simeq 8\times10^{-17}\)
(\texttt{AguilarSaavedra2004:T002:SM}).  The interpretation is
operator-dependent: ATLAS quotes left- and right-handed tensor-SMEFT
benchmarks, while an RS model can generate vector-like \(Ztu\) couplings,
KK-mediated contact effects, or correlated shifts in other electroweak
vertices.  A live constraint therefore needs a matching convention before the
branching-ratio limit can be used as a hard scan cut.

In down-aligned or kaon-protected RS variants, top FCNCs become leading rather
than secondary diagnostics.
sections.code_coverage_prose:
Coverage is \textbf{NO}.  The PKA reran the required plan greps and a targeted
search for \(t\to uZ\), \(tZu\), \(tuZ\), \(tZq\), and generic FCNC text across
\texttt{quarkConstraints/}, \texttt{qcd/}, \texttt{flavorConstraints/},
\texttt{neutrinos/}, \texttt{yukawa/}, \texttt{warpConfig/},
\texttt{solvers/}, \texttt{scanParams/}, and \texttt{tests/}.  The only
targeted code-directory hits were generic FCNC prose/comments, not an
observable implementation, likelihood, or branching-ratio check.
sections.implementation_difficulty_prose:
The cataloging difficulty is medium, but production integration is \textbf{HIGH}.
The repo has no electroweak top-FCNC operator basis, no \(tuZ\) decay-width or
single-production reinterpretation, and no top-sector flavor-rotation
interface.  Implementing it honestly would require new RS-to-SMEFT or direct
\(Ztu\) matching before comparing this limit to scan points.

matches.context:
L18: The paper studies the flavor structure of 5D warped models that provide a
L19: dual description of a composite pseudo-Goldstone Higgs. It re-examines flavor
L20: constraints in standard Randall-Sundrum-type scenarios and finds that the KK
L21: gluon mass should generically be heavier than about 21 TeV. The authors find
L22: additional sources of flavor violation in the composite-Higgs setup, including
L23: kinetic mixing among SM fields, and conclude that fully anarchic warped flavor
L24: is strongly constrained unless partial flavor symmetries remove some sources
